talk_title: Digital Tissue Twins: AI‑Powered Prediction of Single‑Cell Spatial Omics from H&E Slides

models.name: MD Anderson spatial transcriptomics
models.genotype_or_key_features: 23 LUAD patients, medium-resolution spatial gene expression

MOA_summary: Li's group developed AI models (iSTAR, iSCALE, AURORA) that learn the relationship between H&E morphology and spatial gene expression, using bulk RNA‑seq as a dynamic‑range constraint to predict single‑cell resolution transcriptomic maps and cell‑type annotations across whole‑slide images.
key_findings: AURORA achieved the highest Pearson correlation and lowest false‑positive rate among competing methods when predicting gene expression on held‑out LUAD samples (5 patients).
Virtual prediction of 4,000 TCGA LUAD slides reproduced known tumor pathology patterns (e.g., KRT8 expression).
Mature TLS area quantified by AURORA correlated with improved patient survival in TCGA LUAD.
Higher tumor‑TLS co‑localization was associated with better cancer survival.
Spatial cuff structures identified by AURORA predicted poorer survival, recapitulating findings from Wang et al. Cancer Cell.
open_questions: How will AURORA perform on treated or therapy‑resistant tumor samples where dynamic range may shift?
Can virtual predictions be extended to infer subclonal genetic alterations?

talk_title: Rosie: Generative AI for In‑Silico Spatial Proteomics from H&E Slides
MOA_summary: Rosie trains a hierarchical deep‑learning model on paired H&E and CODEX images to impute multiplex protein expression at 8 µm resolution, enabling rapid, low‑cost generation of in‑silico spatial proteomics maps.
key_findings: Rosie generates in‑silico CODEX images for up to 50 protein markers in minutes for <$1 cost, matching key structures seen in experimental CODEX.
Performance across external validation sites remained robust, with Pearson correlations ranging from high (99th percentile) to moderate (25th percentile).
Imputed proteomic maps enable accurate segmentation of tissue structures (stroma, tumor epithelium, endothelium) comparable to ground‑truth CODEX.
open_questions: How does Rosie generalize to novel tissue types or rare biomarkers not represented in the training set?
What are the limits of sub‑cellular resolution for accurate protein quantification?

talk_title: Virtual Biotech and AI‑Driven Spatial Omics for Therapeutic Strategy Design

biomarkers.name: CD276 expression
biomarkers.finding: high CD276 expression in fibroblasts associated with poorer survival and reduced therapy response

models.name: Virtual Biotech AI agents
models.genotype_or_key_features: integrates single‑cell atlases, spatial transcriptomics, genetics, clinical trial data

clinical_data.population: lung cancer patients with high CD276

MOA_summary: The virtual biotech platform uses AI agents to integrate multi‑omic and clinical data, identifying CD276 (B7H3) as a fibroblast‑derived immune checkpoint; agents propose an ADC (CD276‑DXd) strategy, later validated by an FDA breakthrough designation for a similar ADC.
key_findings: AI agents identified CD276 highly expressed in tumor‑associated fibroblasts in lung cancer.
Spatial analysis showed CD276‑high fibroblast neighborhoods depleted of immune cells.
High CD276 expression correlated with poorer overall and disease‑specific survival.
Agents independently designed a CD276‑DXd ADC strategy, later mirrored by Merck's FDA‑breakthrough ADC.

combination_strategies.rationale: Target CD276 on fibroblasts to deliver cytotoxic payload directly to immunosuppressive niche
combination_strategies.phenotype: preclinical efficacy predicted; clinical breakthrough designation achieved

open_questions: Can AI‑derived single‑cell features (tau score, bimodality) predict trial success across other indications?
How robust is the CD276 fibroblast target across diverse ethnic and molecular subpopulations?
